title: TIME IS GETTING NEARER
text:
TIME IS GETTING NEARER 
R) 	Time is getting nearer and nearer, 

Time is getting closer and closer, 

If Jesus should come at the end of this day,

Are you ready for his glorious coming? (2)
1. 	All my people I am speaking to you,

listen to what I am telling you now:

If Jesus should come at the end of this day

Are you ready for His glorious coming?
2. 	My brothers and my sisters, my friends and my foes,

To all I dedicate these words of mine,

To hold so firm till the time,

Till Jesus comes home and takes us away.
3.	 One night I woke up and looked at the sky,

The stars did appear but faded away;

I started to think what was the cause

So fast are the days, are but passing away.

title: EUCHARISTIC ADORATION AND BENEDICTION
text:
EUCHARISTIC ADORATION AND BENEDICTION
Christ promised that he would remain with us till the end of the 
world and He is keeping His promise. He is present in his Word, 
in the priest who presides the Eucharistic celebration, in the celebration of the various sacraments, in the community united 
in his name, in the poor, the sick etc. He is present mainly in 
the Holy Eucharist to be the Bread of life for the sick, for those 
unable to attend Holy Mass and to continue with everyone the 
meeting which started with the Eucharistic celebration. 
Any exposition of the Blessed Sacrament, though short, should 
be ordained in such a way that before the Benediction a convenient time is given to the reading of the Word of God, to hymns, 
prayers and silent adoration.
SCHEME
1. Hymn (while the Blessed Sacrament is exposed).
2. Reading of the Word of God.
(Mc XIV, 17-26). And when it was evening He came with 
the twelve. And as they were at table eating, Jesus said: 
'Truly I say to you, one of you will betray me, one who is 
eating with me'' And as they were eating, he took bread 
and blessed and broke it and gave it to them and said. 
'Take, this is my Body'. And He took the cup and when 
He had given thanks he gave it to them and they all drank 
of it. And he said to them: 'This is my Blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many'' And when they 
had sung a hymn, they went out to the mount of Olives. 
And Jesus said to them: 'You will all fall away, for it is 
written 'I will strike the shepherd and the sheep will be 
scattered''... 
Or (Mt 26, 20-29) ' Or (Lk 22, 14-23) ' Or (1 Cor. 11, 23-30).

title: 3. Personal meditation
text:
3. Personal meditation 
- Jesus is present in the Eucharist not only to be adored or 
feasted, but to be imitated. What he has done for us, must 
be our own project of life: service for the kingdom of God.
- Jesus was betrayed even by his own community. We are 
all sinners, so be vigilant and humble. He however, died 
for this very church, thus trust in Him and be converted. 
This is a time and place for conversion.
- Holy communion is union with Jesus and our brethren, 
is the gift for our salvation, but is also judgement on the 
dispositions of our hearts. 
4. Hymn 	 1. Father, we adore you - Lay out lives 

before you -

How we love you.

2. 	Jesus, we adore you'

3.	 Spirit, we adore you'
Prayer
- For our community. 
- For the universal church. For unity. 
- For vocations.
- For benefactors. 
- others.
5. Hymn 	 (Tantum Ergo)

Come adore this live presence 

Bow to Christ the source of grace. 

Here is kept the ancient promise: 

Earth is now God's dwelling place. 

Eyes can't see God's shining glory,

Faith alone may see his face. 
Glory be to God the Father,

title: Praise to his eternal Son.
text:
Praise to his eternal Son.
Adoration to the spirit,
God of love in loving one.
Blessed be God by every creature,
Ever be his praises sung. Amen
V)	You have given your people bread from heaven.
R) 	The bread which is full of all goodness.

Let us pray.

O God, who in this wonderful Sacrament have left for 
us a memorial of your passion, help us so to reverence 
the sacred mysteries of your Body and Blood, that we 
may always experience in our lives the effects of your 
Redemption, who live and reign for ever and ever 
Amen.

receive the benediction of the most Blessed Sacrament.

Blessed be God in His Angels and in His Saints.

title: A CELEBRATION OF THE WORD
text:
A CELEBRATION OF THE WORD
FOR THE FAITHFUL DEPARTED
1. Hymn 
2. Opening prayer 
(Leader) Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus 
Christ, the Father of mercy and God of all consolation. He 
comforts us in all our afflictions. Come to me all who labour and are heavy laden and I will give you rest.
Psalm 129 (page 201???)
'Out of the depths I cry to you o Lord'
R. I place all my trust in you my God. 
Psalm 22 (page 157???)
'The Lord's my shepherd' (N. 82)
R. The Lord is my shepherd, there is nothing I shall want.
Prayer: Listen to our prayers, o Lord, as we humbly beg 
your mercy. That the soul of your servant N. whom you 
have called from this life may be brought by you to a place 
of peace and light and so be enabled to share the life of all 
your saints. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
3. First Reading
(Romans 14: 7-9. We live and die for Christ).
None of us lives as his own master and none of us dies as 
his own master. While we live we are responsible to the 
Lord and when we die we die as his servants. Both in life 
and in death we are the Lord's. That is why Christ died 
and came to life again, that he might be Lord both of the 
dead and of the living.

text:

To dwell in the house of the Lord all days of my life.

That I may gaze on the loveliness of the Lord and

title: 8. The Lord�s prayer
category: A Celebration of the Word for the Faithful Departed
text:
8. The Lord's prayer 
(Holy Rosary or part of it).
9. Closing Prayer 
Let us pray. Our Lord and Redeemer, you gave yourself 
up to death that all men might be saved and pass from 
death to life. We humbly ask you in your merciful goodness to look upon all who mourn and pray for their dead 
brother (sister). We ask you, Lord, who alone are holy and 
most merciful and who through your own death opened 
the gates of life to your faithful ones, to free him (her) from 
all his (her) sins. Let not our brother (sister) ever be separated from you, o everlasting king. By the power of your 
glory give him (her) a dwelling place of light, happiness 
and peace. You who live and reign for ever.
R. Amen. 
For the mourners 
Grant, o Lord, that while we lament the departure of this 
your servant, we may always remember that we are most 
certainly to follow him (her). 
Give us grace to prepare for that last hour by a good life, 
that we may not be surprised by a sudden and unprovided death: but be ever watching that when you call we may 
enter into eternal glory.
Through Christ our Lord.
At the graveside 
Blessing of the grave
O God, through whose mercy the souls of the faithful are 
at rest, bless (') this grave, we beg you, and send your holy 
angel to guard it. Free from all bonds of sin the soul of him 
(her) whose body is buried here, so that he (she) may find 
joy in your company with all the saints for ever and ever.
Through Christ our Lord. Amen.

title: Prayer
category: A Celebration of the Word for the Faithful Departed

blocks.text: Prayer
We commend to you, o Lord the soul of your servant N.
May he (she), though dead to this world, find his (her) life
forever with you.
Of your merciful goodness forgive him (her) whatever
sins he (she) committed through human frailty. Through
Christ our Lord. Amen.
Eternal rest grant to them o Lord, and let perpetual light
shine upon them.
May they rest in peace. Amen. (Whilst sprinkling Holy Water)
Hymn:
ABIDE WITH ME
1. 	Abide with me; fast falls the eventide;
The darkness deepens; Lord, with me abide;
When other helpers fail and comforts flee,
Help of the helpless, O abide with me.
2. 	Swift to its close ebbs out life�s little day;
Earth�s joy grow dim, its glories pass away;
Change and decay in all around I see;
O thou who changest not, abide with me.
3. 	Hold thou thy cross before my closing eyes;
Shine through the gloom, and point me to the skies;
Heaven�s morning breaks, and earth�s vain shadows flee;
In life, in death, O Lord, abide with me!

title: (Invocations of the Patron Saints of the family).
text:
(Invocations of the Patron Saints of the family).
Priest: O Lord, hear our prayer. 
All: And let our cry come to you. 
All: Let us pray.
O merciful and almighty God, we ask you to bless continually all of us who dwell in this house. May holy water 
repel all evil spirit in the name of Jesus.
Guard the doors of this house that no evil may come in. 
Bless our children that they may grow in wisdom and 
grace. 
Console those who are sick and in sorrow, give eternal rest 
to our dear ones who have died. 
Help us to live an exemplary christian life. We ask this 
through Christ our Lord. Amen.
Our Father - Hail Mary - Glory be.
 (or part of the Rosary)
CHRIST BE BESIDE ME
Christ be beside me, Christ be before me,
Christ be behind me, King of my heart.
Christ be within me, Christ be below me,
Christ be above me, Never to part.
Christ on my right hand, Christ on my left hand,
Christ all around me, shield in the strife.
Christ in my sleeping, Christ in my sitting 
Christ in my rising, Light of my life.
Christ be in all hearts, thinking about me,
Christ be on all tongues telling of me
Christ be the vision in eyes that see me,
In ears that hear me, Christ ever be.

title: A SERVICE OF PRAYER FOR VOCATIONS
text:
A SERVICE OF PRAYER FOR VOCATIONS
Vocation is a call by God. He calls everyone to human life, 
to divine life and to salvation after the fall by sin. (L.G.2).
By Baptism we responded to God's call that we would 
care for our salvation in Christ and would try to be good 
disciples of Jesus in our concern and love for others.
There are, however, specific vocations for which Jesus 
invited his disciples to pray and to strive for, i.e. for the 
priesthood, brotherhood and sisterhood.
Thus some men and women consecrate their entire life and 
talents to the service of God and neighbour. This makes a 
person to imitate Christ to the extent of remaining 'virgin', 
'poor', and 'obedient' as He was in his earthly life.
Christ does not force anyone to follow him in this way; he 
does not appeal to justice or duty, but to love: 'if you want...'.

title: PRAYER WITH THE PSALMS
text:
PRAYER WITH THE PSALMS
The psalms are the prayer of Israel, of Jesus and the 
church. They are simple prayers of very ordinary people. 
These people expressed themselves in their joy, sorrow, 
thanksgiving, adoration and praise. Joy and sorrow of 
every man and woman in the presence of the Emmanuel 
'God with us'.
The writers of the psalms admired God's creation in the 
beauty of nature, the fields, the sky, the birds and animals.
In reading or praying the psalms, we listen to the Word 
of God. We speak to Him in response. We join in prayers 
with others, we share our reflections. We grow together in 
faith and love.
LAUDS
(Morning prayer)
I BELIEVE IN GOD THE FATHER
1. 	I believe in God the Father,

I believe in God his Son,

I believe in God their Spirit 

Each is God yet God is one.

I believe what God has spoken 

Through his church whose word is true,

Boldly she proclaims his Gospel

ever old yet ever new.
2. 	All my hope is in God's goodness 

Shown for us by Him who died,

Jesus Christ the world Redeemer,

Spotless victim crucified.

All my love is love eternal,

In that love I love mankind,

Take my heart, o heart once broken, 

Take my soul, my strength, my mind.

title: 3.	 Father I have sinned against you,
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
3.	 Father I have sinned against you,

Look on me with eyes of love,

Look you wandering sheep, good Shepherd, 

grant heaven's peace, o heavenly dove.
 	 Blessed be God the Loving Father, 

bless'd be God his only Son,
 	 Blessed be God, all Holy Spirit,

bless'd be God for ever more.
PSALM 63 (sul libro ' 69...)
Life begins anew with each day.
First thing seek the Lord. God shows 
us the Way, gives us his blessings, 
and can give life even to dry land.
Ant. Teach me, O Lord, to do your will. 
O Lord, you are my God I seek you,
my soul thirsts for you;
My flesh faints for you as in a dry
and weary land where no water is.
I have looked upon you in the sanctuary 
Beholding you power and glory.
Because your steadfast love is better 
than life, my lips will praise you.
I will bless you as long as I live, 
I will lift up my hands 
And call on your name. 
Glory be'

blocks.text: 3.	 Father I have sinned against you,
Look on me with eyes of love,
Look you wandering sheep, good Shepherd,
grant heaven�s peace, o heavenly dove.
Blessed be God the Loving Father,
bless�d be God his only Son,
Blessed be God, all Holy Spirit,
bless�d be God for ever more.
PSALM 63 (sul libro � 69...)
Life begins anew with each day.
First thing seek the Lord. God shows
us the Way, gives us his blessings,
and can give life even to dry land.
Ant. Teach me, O Lord, to do your will.
O Lord, you are my God I seek you,
my soul thirsts for you;
My flesh faints for you as in a dry
and weary land where no water is.
I have looked upon you in the sanctuary
Beholding you power and glory.
Because your steadfast love is better
than life, my lips will praise you.
I will bless you as long as I live,
I will lift up my hands
And call on your name.
Glory be�

title: CANTICLE OF THE THREE CHILDREN
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
CANTICLE OF THE THREE CHILDREN
When you are happy, you like to express your 
joy in singing.
It is not good to be happy alone. 
No man is an island. Share your happiness and 
invite all the creatures of heaven and earth to 
praise and bless the Lord.
Ant: To Him be highest glory and praise for ever.
 1.	O all you works of the Lord, bless the Lord.
 2. 	And you angels of the Lord, bless the Lord.
 3. 	And you the heavens of the Lord, bless the Lord.
 4. 	And you, sun and moon, bless the Lord.
 5. 	And you, stars of the heav'ns bless the Lord.
 6. 	And you, showers and rain, bless the Lord.
 7. 	And you, all breezes and winds, bless the Lord.
 8. 	And you, cold and heat, bless the Lord.
 9. 	And you, night time and day, bless the Lord.
10. 	And you, mountains and hills, bless the Lord.
11. 	And you, all plants of the earth, bless the Lord.
12. 	And you, rivers and seas, bless the Lord.
13. 	And you, creatures of the sea, bless the Lord.
14. 	And you, ev'ry bird of the sky, bless the Lord.
15. 	And you, wild beasts and tame, bless the Lord.
16. 	And you, children of men, bless the Lord.
17.	And you, priests of the Lord, bless the Lord
18.	And you, servants of the Lord, bless the Lord.
PSALM 15
What makes one a friend of God? Having a pure heart, 
speaking no evil, doing no harm, telling the truth and 	
keeping one's word.
O Lord, who shall sojourn in your tent?

title: Who shall dwell on your holy hill?
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
Who shall dwell on your holy hill?
He who walks blamelessly and does what is right and 
speaks truth from his heart.
He who does not slander with his tongue 
and does no evil with his friends. 
He who is faithful to his promises,
And does not take a bribe against the innocent. 
He who is firm in faith and just in his dealings,
Such is a strong person, always loved by God.
Glory be'
Reading (Tob. 4, 15-17...)
What you hate, do not do to anyone. Give of your bread 
to the hungry and of your clothing to the naked. Give all 
your surplus to charity.
Seek advice from every wise person and do not despise 
any useful counsel. Bless God the Lord on every occasion, 
ask Him that your ways be made straight and that all your 
paths and plans may prosper.
Reflection 
Hymn: SONG OF ZECHARIAH
Ant: Blessed be the Lord, he has visited us and freed us.
Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel!
He has visited his people and redeemed them.
He has raised up for us a mighty saviour
in the house of David his servant, 
as he promised by the lips of holy men, 
those who were his prophets from of old.
A saviour who would free us from our foes,

title: From the hands of all who hate us.
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
From the hands of all who hate us. 
So his love for our fathers if fulfilled 
and his holy covenant remembered. 
He swore to Abraham our father to grant us, 
that free from fear, and saved from the hands of our foes,
we might serve him in holiness and justice 
all the days of our life in his presence.
As for you, little child,
you shall be called a prophets of God, the Most High,
You shall go ahead of the Lord 
to prepare his ways before him. 
To make known to his people their salvation 
through forgiveness of all their sins,
the loving-kindness of the heart of our God
who visits us like the dawn from on high.
He will give light to those in darkness,
those who dwell in the shadow of death, 
and guide us into the way of peace.
Prayers 
Leader: 	 Let us turn to God, who is the true sun which 
 	 never sets, the light that enlightens every
 	 man, who comes into this world.
All: 	
Lord, you are my life and salvation.
Leader: 	 May your spirit teach us to do your will today.
All: 	
And may your wisdom guide us.
Leader: 	 We give you thanks from our hearts.
All: 	
For all your blessings towards us. 
Leader: 	 Defend our tongue from evil 
All: 	
So that we may all day praise and bless you
Leader: 	 Help us to live in charity with one another 
All: 	 And never render evil for evil.
 	 (other prayers)
Our Father'

title: CONCLUDING PRAYERS
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
CONCLUDING PRAYERS
May the Lord bless us, 
may he keep us from all evil and lead us to life everlasting. 
Amen.
Leader: Let us bless the Lord. 
All: Thanks be to God.
VESPERS
(Evening prayer)
(This Can also be used for reconciliation and funeral services)
It is evening. The day is over. Let us reflect on our day: 
our work, the people we have met, our pleasures and 
our difficulties. 
We thank God for sparing us, giving us the opportunity to help others and ask Him to pardon us for the 
times we have offended him or refused to help somebody in need. We know, Lord, that you are ready to 
forgive us and help us.
Leader:	 O Lord come to our aid.
All:	
O Lord, make haste to help us. 

Glory be'
HYMN
Day is done, but love unfailing, dwells ever here,
Shadows fall, but hope prevailing, calms every fear. 
Loving Father, none forsaking,
Take our hearts, of love's own making,
Watch our sleeping, guard our waking. Be always near.
Dark descends, but light unending 
Shines through the night,
You are with us, ever lending 
New strength to sight.

title: One in Love, your truth confessing,
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
One in Love, your truth confessing,
One in hope of heaven's blessing, 
May we see, in love's possessing,
Love's endless light. 
Eyes will close, but you unsleeping, 
Watch by our side,
Death may come: in Love's safe keeping 
Still we abide.
God of love, all evil quelling 
Sin forgiving, fear dispelling, 
Stay with us, our hearts indwelling.
This eventide.
PSALM 129
You may be discouraged by your weakness and failures.
Do not hesitate to cry to the Lord out of the depths of 
your confusion. Trust in Him.
Ant: I place all my trust in you, my God; all my hope is in 
your saving word.
1. 	Out of the depths I cry to you, o Lord,

Lord, hear my voice!

O let your ear be attentive

To the voice of my pleading.
2. 	If you, O Lord, should mark our guilt, 

Lord, who would survive?

But with you is found forgiveness: 

For this we revere you.
3. 	My soul is waiting for the Lord,

I count on his word:

My soul is waiting for the Lord,

more than watchman for daybreak.

title: 4. 	Because with the Lord there is mercy
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
4. 	Because with the Lord there is mercy

and fullness of Redemption,

Israel indeed he will redeem 

from all its iniquity.
5. 	To the Father Almighty give glory,

give glory to his Son,

to the Spirit most holy give praise, 

whose reign is forever.
PSALM 50
In the world there is much evil: wars, hatred, injustices, racism, etc. Where is its source? It is very near me, 
in me, in my selfishness, in my pretence to be always 
better and right.
To seek happiness away from God is a delusion. Peace 
and joy go from us when the Grace of God is lost.
Ant: Have mercy, Lord, free me from all my sins.
Have mercy on me, God, in your kindness. 
In your compassion blot out my offence.
O wash me more and more from my guilt
and cleanse me from my sin.
My offences truly I know them;
my sin is always before me.
Against you, you alone, have I sinned;
what is evil in your sight I have done.
That you may be justified when you give sentence 
and be without reproached when you judge.
O see, in guilt I was born,
a sinner was I conceived. 
Indeed you love truth in the heart;
then in the secret of my heart teach me wisdom.

title: O purify me, then I shall be clean;
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
O purify me, then I shall be clean;
O wash me, I shall be whiter than snow. 
Make me hear rejoicing and gladness, 
that the bones you have crushed may revive.
From my sins turn away your face 
And blot out all my guilt.
A pure heart create for me, O God,
put a steadfast spirit within me.
Do not cast me away from your presence,
nor deprive me of your holy spirit.
Give me again the joy of your help;
with a spirit of fervor sustain me,
that I may teach transgressors your ways
and sinners may return to you.
Give glory to the Father almighty,
To his Son, Christ the Lord,
To the spirit who dwell in our hearts,
Both now and for ever. Amen.
PSALM 145
Our salvation is in God alone
Ant: I will praise my God all my days.
I will praise my God all my days.
My soul, give praise to the Lord;
I will praise the Lord all my days,
make music to my God while I live.
Put no trust in princes,
in mortal men in whom there is no help. 
Take their breath, they return to clay
and their plans that day come to nothing.

title: He is happy who is helped by Jacob�s God,
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
He is happy who is helped by Jacob's God,
whose hope is in the Lord his God,
who alone made heaven and earth, 
the seas and all they contain.
It is he who keeps faith for ever,
who is just to those who are oppressed.
It is he who gives bread to the hungry,
the Lord, who sets prisoners free.
The Lord who gives sight to the blind,
who raises up those who are bowed down,
the Lord, who protects the stranger
and upholds the widow and orphan.
It is the Lord who loves the just
but thwarts the path of the wicked. 
The Lord will reign for ever,
Zion's God, from age to age.
READINGS
1. 	1 Cor. 13. There are three things that last: faith, hope 
and love. And the greatest gift is the gift of love. If I 
speak the tongue of an angel but I do not love, I am just 
a voice making noise, I am nothing at all.
2.	 Eph. 4:26-27. Do not sin: do not let the sun go down on 
your anger and give no opportunity to the devil.
3.	 1 Pet. 5:6-7 Humble yourselves under the right hand of 
God that in due time he may exalt you. 

Cast all your anxieties in Him, for He cares about you.
Reflection 
Canticle of Our Lady (Magnificat, see N. 44)

title: PRAYERS
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
PRAYERS

the living.
All: 	 Into your hands, o Lord, I commend my spirit. 
Leader: 	 Lord, you sent your Son to save sinners.
All: 	 Grant to all the dead the forgiveness of their sins.

Concluding prayer
Stay with us o Lord, for it is nearly evening, Enkindle our 
heart and stir up our hope so that we and all our fellow 
men and women may learn to know you in the Scriptures 
and in the Breaking of Bread: you live and reign for ever 
and ever.
May the Lord bless us. May he keep us from all evil and 
lead us to life everlasting. Amen.
Leader: 	 Let us bless the Lord.
All: 	
Thanks be to God.

title: PRAYING WITH THE BIBLE (LECTIO DIVINA)
category: Prayer with the Psalms (Lauds and Vespers)
text:
PRAYING WITH THE BIBLE (LECTIO DIVINA)
Whether you are praying alone or with others, you may pray 
with the Bible, in various ways: reciting, reflecting singing.
The Seven Steps: is a simple method developed by a team of 
experts.
1. 	We invite the Lord: a brief prayer by one of the members, 
to be open to the presence of God.
2. 	We read the text: one volunteer reads the chosen text aloud. 
3. 	We look at the text again: each member is invited to read 
aloud a word or phrase which struck him/her, followed by a 
silent pause. 
4. 	We let God speak to us: the passage is read again slowly, 
followed by a silence of 2-5 minutes.
After listening and letting the Word sink in our hearts and 
minds:
5. 	We share what we have heard in our hearts and minds, 
with no discussions or preaching.
6. 	We search together: we examine our lives, our daily problems in the light of the Gospel.
7. 	We pray together: everyone is invited to pray in his/her 
personal words.

title: 122b 	 Glory dae to God (Glory)
